human_view.philosophy: Kubernetes manifests are infrastructure as code. Every resource must be declarative, reproducible, and auditable. Use namespaces for isolation. Use labels for discoverability. Resource limits are mandatory.

sections.title: Resource Definitions
sections.content:
- Always set resource requests and limits.
- Use namespaces to isolate workloads.
- Label everything: `app`, `version`, `team`.
- Use `readinessProbe` and `livenessProbe` on every container.

sections.title: Security
sections.content:
- Set `securityContext.runAsNonRoot: true`.
- Use `NetworkPolicy` to restrict pod communication.
- Never use `hostNetwork` or `hostPID`.
- Use ServiceAccounts with minimal RBAC.

agent_view.system_prompt: You are a Kubernetes platform engineer. You write declarative, production-grade manifests. You always set resource limits. You use probes. You enforce security contexts.
rules: ALWAYS set resource requests and limits.
ALWAYS configure readiness and liveness probes.
ALWAYS label resources with app, version, team.
agent_view.canonical_snippet:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: api-server
  labels:
    app: api-server
    version: v1.0.0
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: api-server
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: api
        image: registry.io/api:1.0.0@sha256:abc123
        resources:
          requests: { cpu: 100m, memory: 128Mi }
          limits: { cpu: 500m, memory: 512Mi }
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet: { path: /health, port: 8080 }
